name: Aubergine with Pork & Prawns (Makhua Phao Song Kruang)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 230°C (450°F/Gas Mark 8). Roast the aubergines whole until the skins are burnt, or grill them directly over strong heat.
2. Remove the aubergines from the oven and toss them in cold water. Peel and cut into large pieces, then place in a serving dish.
3. In a bowl, mix together the pork, prawns, pepper, shallots, garlic, vinegar, fish sauce, fermented soya beans, and sugar.
4. Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the shallots and cook until golden brown. Remove the shallots and set aside with the oil.
5. Add the garlic to the pan and cook until golden brown. Stir in the pork and prawn mixture, then sauté for a few minutes.
6. Pour in the vinegar, fish sauce, fermented soya beans, and sugar. Mix well and cover.
7. Cook for a few more minutes until everything is heated through.
8. Arrange the aubergine pieces on serving plates and top with the pork and prawn mixture. Sprinkle with additional sugar to taste.
This recipe was originally shared by Stephen Ceideburg in "Discover Thai Cooking" (1987).
